title: brain evidence resolve <evidenceId> — --json success output
description: Deterministically reverify a flat v2 vault-derived evidence row by re-anchoring it to the CURRENT state of its note. Evidence is vault-authoritative: the resolution is NOT a direct SQLite/ledger write — a `resolved` outcome writes status/verdict/lastCheckedAt into the note's `evidence:` frontmatter entry through the canonical mutation order (one commit onto refs/heads/main, then re-fold), so it lives in canonical Markdown and survives a rebuild. A note (or its frontmatter entry) that no longer resolves is `outcome: target-missing`, `status: needs-review`, no mutation, exit 0 — never a crash. No renditions, no run-id, no ledger-seq, no audit signature (ADR-0003). A dirty target note is refused by the dirty-vault gate (exit 2). Missing evidence ⇒ not-found (exit 1).

allOf.description: outcome=resolved ⇒ status=resolved AND a canonical commit (the Markdown SSOT write).
outcome=target-missing ⇒ status=needs-review, no commit (nothing was mutated).

outcome.description: resolved = the note + its frontmatter entry resolve, status written to Markdown + committed; target-missing = the soft noteId/entry no longer resolves, no mutation.

noteId.description: The soft-referenced owning note (`(unknown)` when the row carries no noteId).

status.description: The evidence status after resolution, bound to outcome: resolved ⇔ resolved; needs-review ⇔ target-missing.

commit.description: 40-char hex canonical commit the resolution landed as (the Markdown SSOT write). Present ONLY on outcome=resolved.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Deterministically reverify a flat evidence row against its note; a resolved outcome writes status/verdict to the note frontmatter through the mutation order (one commit); a gone target is target-missing (needs-review, no mutation).

args.description: The evidence id to reverify (from `brain evidence review`).

sideEffects: on outcome=resolved: writes status/verdict/lastCheckedAt into the note's evidence frontmatter and commits it onto refs/heads/main via the canonical mutation order, then re-folds the projection
prohibitedEffects: never writes the evidence status directly to SQLite bypassing the note-frontmatter mutation order (would be lost on db rebuild)
no run-id / ledger-seq / audit signature read or write
never mutates a gone target note

locks.when: outcome=resolved (the mutation order holds the vault lock)

errorCodes.when: the evidence id does not exist
the vault repo is missing/unreadable, HEAD is not main, or the target note is dirty
